problem.title: Counting Sort
problem.description: Implement counting sort algorithm for sorting integers with a known range.
assumptions: Works best when values are in a known, bounded range.

example.explanation: Count occurrences of each value, then reconstruct sorted array. O(n+k) time.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Counting Sort Implementation
solutions.description: Count the frequency of each distinct value, convert counts to cumulative positions (prefix sums), then place each element in its correct output position by reading the original array right-to-left to preserve stability. Because it uses no comparisons, counting sort runs in O(n+k) time where k is the value range.
